disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No cauda equina features — no urinary retention/incontinence, no faecal incontinence, no saddle (perineal/buttock) numbness, no progressive/bilateral leg weakness or numbness
trigger_question_examples: Any loss of control of your bladder or bowels, difficulty passing urine, or numbness around the groin/buttocks/inner thighs?
Any weakness or numbness spreading in both legs?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — normal bladder and bowel control, no numbness around the groin, and my legs feel normal with no weakness.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No other serious-cause red flags — no significant trauma/osteoporosis (fracture), no fever/IV drug use/immunosuppression (infection), no history of cancer/unexplained weight loss/night pain (malignancy), age <50, no thoracic pain
trigger_question_examples: Any major injury/fall, fevers, unexplained weight loss, night pain, or a history of cancer?
Any immune problems or IV drug use?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No major injury, no fevers, no weight loss or night pain, no cancer history or immune problems.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No radicular (sciatica) pain radiating below the knee in a dermatomal pattern
trigger_question_examples: Does the pain shoot down your leg past the knee, or stay in the back??
Can you tell me a bit more about that?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It stays in my back — it doesn't shoot down my leg.
